VAL-SEC-C1.evidence: Code inspection: mint.ts uses fastify.prisma.$transaction with conditional updateMany({where: {id, status: 'DRAFT'}}) to atomically check and update status, preventing TOCTOU. ADMIN role is permitted via requireRole('BRAND_ADMIN','ADMIN'). Concurrent test: Created DRAFT product 'C1-TOCTOU Test Product' (id: cmmbswz470008wl14rz99pbhl), sent two simultaneous POST /products/:id/mint requests. Result: one returned HTTP 200 (product minted to ACTIVE), the other returned HTTP 409 with {success:false, error:{code:'CONFLICT', message:'Product is not in DRAFT status'}}. Exactly one succeeded, confirming race condition is fixed.
steps: POST /auth/login as admin@galileo.test — HTTP 200, role=ADMIN, brandId=cmmbsu3fv00000114e4js4wji

POST /products with gtin=00012345678905, serialNumber=C1-TOCTOU-001, name='C1-TOCTOU Test Product', category=Watches, brandId=cmmbsu3fv00000114e4js4wji — HTTP 201, product id=cmmbswz470008wl14rz99pbhl, status=DRAFT
Launched two concurrent POST /products/cmmbswz470008wl14rz99pbhl/mint requests (background curl processes)
Request A: HTTP 200 — product minted successfully, status=ACTIVE, txHash and tokenAddress populated
Request B: HTTP 409 — {success:false, error:{code:'CONFLICT', message:'Product is not in DRAFT status'}}
GET /products/cmmbswz470008wl14rz99pbhl — confirmed product status is ACTIVE
Code inspection: mint.ts line 49 uses $transaction wrapping findUnique + updateMany({where:{id,status:'DRAFT'}}) ensuring atomic check-and-update

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-SEC-C1 (mint TOCTOU race condition) confirmed fixed — concurrent mint requests correctly produce exactly one 200 and one 409.
